pipeline_architecture.name: RFPipe
pipeline_architecture.description: Pipeline-style Random Forest classifier with functional composition

outputs.description: Function that generates numerical features from an RFPipe

generate_numerical_features.description: Generate numerical features using mathematical transformations

outputs.description: Function that generates categorical features from an RFPipe

generate_categorical_features.description: Encode categorical features using various encoding methods

outputs.description: Function that generates interaction features from an RFPipe

generate_interaction_features.description: Create interaction features between column pairs

outputs.description: Function that creates binary labels from an RFPipe

create_binary_labels.description: Create binary classification labels based on conditions

outputs.description: Function that creates multiclass labels from an RFPipe

create_multiclass_labels.description: Create multiclass labels by binning continuous variables

outputs.description: Function that selects features from an RFPipe

select_features.description: Select top k features using statistical tests

outputs.description: Function that trains a Random Forest from an RFPipe

train_random_forest.description: Train a Random Forest classifier with specified parameters

outputs.description: Function that saves a model from an RFPipe

save_model.description: Save trained model to disk using joblib

outputs.description: Function that makes predictions from an RFPipe
Function that calculates metrics from an RFPipe

calculate_metrics.description: Calculate classification metrics (accuracy, precision, recall, f1)

outputs.description: Function that prints metrics from an RFPipe

print_metrics.description: Print formatted classification metrics and feature importance

pipeline_data_structure.data: Main DataFrame with features and labels

example_complete_pipeline: RFPipe.from_dataframe(df) | generate_numerical_features(['age', 'income']) | generate_categorical_features(['category']) | create_binary_labels('target', '>100') | select_features(k=10) | train_random_forest(n_estimators=200) | predict() | calculate_metrics() | print_metrics()
